decks.notes: The deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to generate a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. It includes cards that support the combo such as Fleeting Spirit and Guardian of New Benalia, as well as removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse to control the board. The deck also includes mana fixing lands like Godless Shrine and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth to ensure the combo can be assembled quickly and efficiently. Overall, the key concept revolves around setting up the combo and disrupting the opponent's strategy.

The main focus of this UW Control deck is to utilize card advantage, removal spells, and counter spells to control the game and out-value the opponent. With a focus on controlling the board with cards like Supreme Verdict and Memory Deluge, and dealing with threats efficiently through cards like Dovin's Veto and Teferi, Hero of Dominaria, the deck aims to stall the game until it can establish a dominant position. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional value and flexibility in the late game.

The deck focuses on utilizing Greasefang Parhelion to create a powerful combo that overwhelms the opponent. It includes cards like Bitter Triumph, Cache Grab, and Thoughtseize to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Cards like Esika's Chariot and Formidable Speaker help generate tokens and provide additional threats. The deck also includes powerful creatures like Overlord of the Balemurk and Skysovereign, Consul Flagship to further pressure the opponent. With a mix of removal, card draw, and powerful creatures, the deck aims to quickly establish board dominance and win the game.

This deck focuses on utilizing the combo between Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to achieve victory. By using cards like Formidable Speaker and Thoughtseize to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy, as well as utilizing cards like Bitter Triumph and Cache Grab for additional support, the deck aims to create a powerful synergy between its key cards. Skysovereign, Consul Flagship and Overlord of the Balemurk provide additional threats and support for the combo, while cards like Esika's Chariot and Raffine's Informant offer versatility and options for different game states.

This deck focuses on using Greasefang, Okiba Boss along with Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that overwhelms the opponent. The deck includes cards to support and protect the combo, as well as ways to disrupt the opponent's strategy through cards like Thoughtseize and Witherbloom Command. The goal is to quickly assemble the combo pieces while controlling the game and ultimately winning decisively. The deck also includes options for card advantage and flexibility to handle different situations, making it a versatile and powerful choice for Pioneer 1 vs 1 matches.

This deck aims to quickly ramp up mana using Lotus Field and Crumbling Vestige, then use Scapeshift to search for specific lands to combo off and win the game. The deck also includes some control elements like Arboreal Grazer and Lumra, Bellow of the Woods to stall opponents. The win condition is usually either casting Thassa's Oracle with a small library size or generating massive mana with Boseiju, Who Endures and casting a lethal spell like Culling Ritual. Overall, the deck focuses on efficiently assembling key pieces and executing a game-winning combo.

The main concept of this deck is to aggressively deal damage to the opponent using cheap and efficient burn spells like Burst Lightning and Lightning Strike, while utilizing powerful creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage. The inclusion of cards like Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provide additional sources of damage, while cards like Reckless Rage and Monstrous Rage help to control the board and remove threats. This deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a fast-paced, high-impact offensive strategy.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent using low-cost creatures and burn spells. The goal is to apply constant pressure and force the opponent to react defensively, while utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage to efficiently deal damage. With cards like Burst Lightning and Lightning Strike, along with the ability to utilize lands like Ramunap Ruins for additional burn damage, this deck aims to win the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize.

The main focus of this deck is to maintain control over the game by utilizing a combination of card draw, counter spells, and removal. The deck aims to outvalue and outlast opponents through the use of efficient creatures like Cori-Steel Cutter and Gran-Gran, paired with spells like Abandon Attachments and Boomerang Basics to disrupt opponents' game plan. The deck also has flexibility with cards like Divide by Zero and Combustion Technique to deal with a variety of threats. Overall, the strategy revolves around controlling the board and wearing down opponents over time.

The focus of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with spells and cards, in order to bring back Arclight Phoenix from the graveyard repeatedly for aggressive plays. Using card draw and cheap spells like Opt, Consider, and Sleight of Hand, the deck aims to power out the Phoenix as quickly as possible. With additional removal in Lightning Axe and Torch the Tower, the deck looks to control the board while pressuring the opponent with the recurring Phoenix. The mana base is constructed to support the deck's game plan of fueling the graveyard and commanding the battlefield.

This Orzhov Control deck focuses on using powerful removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. It also utilizes hand disruption cards such as Duress and Thoughtseize to further disrupt the opponent's game plan. With the addition of powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Gifted Aetherborn, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and grind them out over the course of the game. Additionally, the deck utilizes mana-fixing lands like Godless Shrine and Mutavault to ensure smooth mana development.

The main focus of this deck is to aggressively control the board and disrupt your opponent's strategy using a combination of powerful creatures, hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Duress, and removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse. The deck also includes cards like Nashi, Searcher in the Dark and Raffine, Scheming Seer to provide card advantage and maintain pressure on your opponent. Overall, this Esper Aggro deck aims to apply early pressure, disrupt the opponent's game plan, and close out the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize.

The main concept of this Orzhov Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan through a combination of hand disruption spells like Duress and Thoughtseize, removal spells like Fatal Push and Go for the Throat, and powerful creatures like Gifted Aetherborn and Preacher of the Schism. The deck aims to control the board and grind out value over time, eventually overwhelming the opponent with a combination of threats and removal. Additionally, cards like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Vanishing Verse provide additional late-game power and flexibility to the deck.

This Red Deck Wins aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming opponents with low-cost, aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage. Utilizing efficient burn spells like Lightning Strike and Burst Lightning, along with powerful enchantments like Monstrous Rage and Reckless Rage, this deck aims to deal damage swiftly and efficiently. The inclusion of resilient lands like Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins ensures a steady stream of threats, backed up by a relentless offense that can often overpower opponents before they have a chance to stabilize.

This aggressive Red Deck Wins archetype focuses on quick, efficient damage dealing to overwhelm opponents. Cards like Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Emberheart Challenger provide early pressure, while spells like Burst Lightning and Lightning Strike help remove threats and deliver direct damage. Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provide additional finishers, while Reckless Rage and Monstrous Rage offer removal and pump effects. With a low curve and a focus on burning down the opponent's life total, this deck aims to win quickly and decisively.

The deck focuses on utilizing low-cost creatures and efficient removal spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive attacks. By playing cheap creatures like Ornithopter and Novice Inspector alongside powerful cards like Venerated Loxodon and Warden of the Inner Sky, the deck aims to establish board presence early and maintain pressure throughout the game. With access to cards like Gleeful Demolition and Cosmogrand Zenith for removal and reach, the Boros Aggro archetype seeks to close out games swiftly before the opponent can stabilize.
